title: Amalfi Yacht Charter Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Planning a yacht charter in Amalfi along the stunning Amalfi Coast means setting off from Marina di Praia, with half-day crewed rates typically between EUR 1,200 and EUR 3,500, and checking verified options on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Amalfi

Set off from one of four real departure points: Marina di Praia, Porto di Amalfi, Marina di Positano or Salerno harbour.

Marina di Praia is a small natural harbour perfect for day boats, while Porto di Amalfi handles larger yachts up to 30 metres. To the west, Marina di Positano offers deep-water access and scenic views of the town stacked on cliffs. Salerno harbour, a 20-minute drive east, has full fuelling facilities and winter storage. Near Li Galli islands, you can anchor off the protected marine reserve. The best strategy to hire a boat involves comparing departure points and season availability.

Which boat to rent in Amalfi

For coastal cruising choose a motor yacht; for island hopping pick a catamaran; for luxury opt for a crewed Azimut.

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 58), ideal for day cruises along the coast to Positano; sleeps 10 guests; from EUR 2,500 per day. See the Azimut 58 for hire with a flybridge and twin engines.
  2. Sailing yacht (e.g. Dufour 56), good for couples or small groups; up to 8 guests; from EUR 1,800 per day with skipper included.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 52), stable for families; up to 12 guests; from EUR 3,000 per day; great for Li Galli islands and Amalfi’s coves.
  4. Rib or tender (e.g. Axopar 37), fast transfers to hidden beaches; up to 8 guests; from EUR 800 half-day.
  5. Luxury crewed yacht (e.g. Maiora 34m), all-inclusive with captain and chef; up to 14 guests; from EUR 6,000 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Amalfi?

Shared tours start at EUR 80 per person; private motorboats from EUR 600 for half a day; catamarans from EUR 2,500 full-day; crewed yachts from EUR 4,000.

  • Shared tour: EUR 80, 150 per person for a 4-hour group trip from Amalfi to Grotta dello Smeraldo.
  • Small motorboat half-day: EUR 600, 900 for a 6-metre boat, no licence required for some models under 40 hp.
  • Catamaran full-day: EUR 2,500, 3,500 for a 45-foot Lagoon; includes skipper and fuel.
  • Crewed yacht: EUR 4,000, 8,000 per day for a 20-metre Sunseeker with crew.
  • Add EUR 200, 400 for extras like paddleboards, water toys or a chef.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Amalfi?

Italian law requires a licence for most motorboats over 40 hp; sailing yachts and small outboards may not need one, but the operator confirms exact requirements.

In high season (July to August), avoid the Scirocco wind which can kick up afternoon swell; set off early to explore Fiordo di Furore before 11am.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, 8,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 4 hours to full-day
  • Capacity: 1 to 14 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Typical distance: Amalfi to Li Galli islands is 14 nautical miles

Questions about boat rental in Amalfi

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Amalfi?

Yes, you can hire a small outboard motorboat (under 40 hp) or a sailing yacht with no licence, though a brief safety briefing is standard.

What is the best month for a yacht charter on the Amalfi Coast?

The best months are May, June and September, when the weather is warm but crowds are thinner and the water is calm.

How many people can a typical crewed yacht hold?

A typical 15-metre crewed yacht in Amalfi accommodates up to 10 guests overnight, with day trips for up to 12 passengers.

Is it cheaper to rent a boat in Amalfi than in Capri?

Yes, hiring a boat from Amalfi is often 10 to 15 percent cheaper than from Capri because base ports are less expensive.
